title: [Bug] Trade log grows unbounded — memory leak in long-running engine
body:
## Problem

The engine appends every trade to `e.trades` and never removes them:

```go
e.trades = append(e.trades, trades...)
```

`GetTrades()` copies the entire slice on every call. For a long-running engine processing millions of trades, this causes:
- Unbounded memory growth
- Increasingly expensive `GetTrades()` calls (copies entire history)
- Eventually OOM crash

## Affected Files
- `pkg/engine/engine.go` — `trades []model.Trade` field, `GetTrades()`

## Proposed Fix
- Add a trade event callback/channel so consumers can process trades in real-time
- Limit the in-memory trade log to a configurable size (ring buffer)
- Or remove the trade log entirely and let callers handle trade storage via the callback

```go
type Engine struct {
    // ...
    onTrade func(symbol string, trade model.Trade)
}
```

## Priority
Medium — won't affect short demos but is a production blocker.
